g.text: How does the extension know when a terminal install finishes?
First to detect the install wins → shuts down the others → rescans all skill folders → updates the UI
Problem: skills.sh has no install library we
can call from code — only a command-line tool
Solution: We run the command in a visible VS Code
terminal so the user can see what's happening
— we have no way to know when they finish
Problem: We need to know when the install
finishes, but there's no callback from the terminal
Solution: Race three listeners in parallel:
1. Terminal Watcher — catches the command exit
2. File Watcher — detects when the install writes
to a tracking file on disk
3. 30s Timeout — gives up and warns the user
Why three? No single method works everywhere.
The terminal watcher needs a newer VS Code. The
Windows. The timeout is a safety net.
Problem: All three listeners could fire for the
same install — we only want to react once
Solution: A simple true/false flag lets the first
listener win and shuts down the other two
Result: Progress bar closes → extension rescans
